evals.expected_output: Factory function returning an interface, closure-encapsulated state, no `class`, no `this`, no `new`. Should explain that the class is replaced because there's no SDK or framework requirement.

assertions.text: no class keyword in the output
returns an object with the API methods

evals.expected_output: Both functions return Result<T, E> instead of throwing. Error type is a discriminated union with specific variants (e.g., 'empty_input' | 'invalid_json' | 'fs_error'). loadConfig handles fs errors. Caller-side example shows pattern-matched handling.

assertions.text: parseConfig signature returns a Result type
Result type is generic with two type params
no throw statements in the refactored code
error type is structured (object with type field), not bare Error

evals.expected_output: Refactored to immutable updates: addItem and updateItem take items as a param and return new arrays via spread / map. State management is explicit (caller owns the array). No mutation of input arguments.

assertions.text: addItem takes items as a parameter (no module-scope mutable state)
addItem returns a new array via spread
updateItem uses map (not Object.assign) to apply updates
